channel.title: Mike McMahon
channel.description: Infrastructure engineer, AI practitioner, Homelab builder, Dad-joke Deliveryman. Documenting the work.

item.title: Credits
item.description: The people who made this journey possible.

item.title: How I Got Here
item.description: From a Commodore 64 in 1983 to infrastructure engineer and AI practitioner — the long road.

item.title: Project: Agent Eval Harness
item.description: A fixed scenario suite with Prometheus instrumentation for comparing LLM models on real infrastructure automation tasks.

item.title: Project: Agent Lab — Phase 1
item.description: The foundation: a controlled infrastructure execution agent with LLM-assisted diagnosis, strict guardrails, and Prometheus observability.

item.title: The Plan: AI Implementation Architect Portfolio
item.description: A structured 4-project lab plan targeting AI Implementation Architect / Staff Engineer readiness — mapped directly to Nate Jones's 7 employer-cited AI skills.

item.title: Project: Failure Detection Dashboard
item.description: Infrastructure observability for AI agent failures — 6 failure modes instrumented via Prometheus/Grafana, cost analysis of OCR model selection, runbooks for diagnosis.

item.title: Project: Multi-Agent Infrastructure Automation
item.description: A 3-agent Planner/Terraform/Validator pipeline for Proxmox homelab automation with LLM-powered failure diagnosis. Zero LLM calls on clean runs.

item.title: Project: OpenBrain
item.description: A personal and family RAG knowledge system — Supabase pgvector backend, Vercel API layer, MCP-integrated into Claude Code sessions.

item.title: Session: Building an Agent Eval Harness
item.description: A fixed scenario suite with Prometheus instrumentation and baseline JSON output for comparing LLM models on real infrastructure automation tasks.

item.title: Building OpenBrain: A Personal RAG Knowledge System
item.description: Standing up a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system backed by Pinecone, hosted on Vercel, and wired into Claude Code sessions.

item.title: Session: Context Preservation Across AI Sessions
item.description: Building the memory and session-wrap infrastructure that makes long-running AI-assisted projects tractable.

item.title: Session: Ingestion Efficiency — From Flat Text to Structured Markdown
item.description: How a Nate B. Jones video on token burn sent me down the rabbit hole of fixing OpenBrain's ingest pipeline — PDF classification, vision OCR, structured chunking, and a 5-model eval.

item.title: Session: Multi-Agent Infrastructure Automation
item.description: Building a 3-agent Planner/Terraform/Validator pipeline that diagnoses its own failures using LLM reasoning.

item.title: Session: Security Hardening the Homelab
item.description: Reviewing and remediating security findings across the homelab — secrets exposure, network segmentation, and Claude session controls.
